mechanism_narrative: A downward drift in FEDFUNDS over the prior three months signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS has declined versus its 3-month average, or rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent 3-month average signals labor-market deterioration that weakens earnings expectations and risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points above its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in at least two-thirds of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand acceleration in sales and residential activity, which improves earnings breadth and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS above its 3-month trend and HOUST above its 3-month trend does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO below its recent trend signals industrial slowdown and weaker corporate revenue leverage, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 1-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if a below-trend INDPRO reading is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the short-horizon effect is not negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A softening in GDPC1 growth combined with weak PAYEMS growth signals broad final-demand deceleration that should lower equity expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not yield lower 60-day SPX returns than either weak GDP or weak payrolls alone, or if the interaction term has no stable negative sign across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near the effective lower bound, investors infer that real rates will stay negative and policy support will persist, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.75% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation trend is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and rising-inflation condition does not improve 20-day SPX returns relative to unconditional baseline, or if the effect reverses when FEDFUNDS is above 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend indicates worsening labor income momentum and weaker household spending capacity, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal's rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST above trend reflects housing-led demand recovery and stronger multiplier effects on consumption and investment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above its 6-month moving average and rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend HOUST periods do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than the base rate, or if the effect disappears after controlling for recent market trend.
